pi_items.source: Queried the RCSB PDB API for entry 4HHB and downloaded the file. Verified its title corresponds to human deoxyhaemoglobin.

pi_items.metadata_value: UniProt hemoglobin beta subunit (8 diverse species including Human P68871)
pi_items.verified_value: UniProt hemoglobin beta subunit (8 diverse species including Human P68871)
pi_items.source: Examined the hemoglobin_beta_multispecies.fasta file which contains exactly 8 sequences formatted with UniProt headers, including >sp|P68871|HBB_HUMAN which matches the UniProt API.
Counted the residues in chain B and D of the downloaded 4HHB.pdb file, which yields 146 residues. 140 is a valid lower bound.
Counted the residues in chain B and D of the downloaded 4HHB.pdb file, which yields 146 residues. 150 is a valid upper bound.
The reference FASTA contains 8 sequences, and adding the extracted PDB sequence makes exactly 9 sequences required in the combined alignment.
Verified via the setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which explicitly create and export from this directory.
Checked the UniProt ID for the human hemoglobin beta sequence in the reference FASTA and verifier script.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real biological data, specifically the RCSB PDB file 4HHB (Human Deoxyhemoglobin) and a FASTA file of 8 UniProt hemoglobin beta subunit sequences, including the human reference P68871. The extracted PDB beta chain has exactly 146 residues, successfully satisfying the expected length bounds (140-150). The final alignment is confirmed to expect exactly 9 sequences. All outputs are generated into the designated /home/ga/UGENE_Data/pdb_cross_alignment directory.
